customer_vision: Blue Yonder wants to be the AI-powered supply chain platform of record for Asia-Pacific. This Fabric implementation is the data foundation for their AI roadmap.
business_objectives: Unified data platform live with all 6 legacy sources migrated
Executive Power BI suite replacing 40 disparate reports
Purview data catalog with 80%+ asset coverage
success_criteria: OneLake ingesting all 6 legacy data sources
Power BI certified dataset adopted by 200+ analysts
risks: Data quality issues in legacy ERP will delay pipeline development
Only 1 internal data engineer — key person risk
pitfalls: Client previously tried a Databricks implementation — abandoned after 6 months due to skills gap
Stakeholder alignment on data ownership is weak
key_decisions: OneLake over Synapse Analytics — better Fabric native integration
Purview for governance from day 1, not as an afterthought
delivery_notes: Legacy ERP data quality is worse than disclosed during pre-sales. Build in a 3-week data profiling sprint before any pipeline work. The 1 internal data engineer (Raj Patel) is excellent — leverage him heavily.
